nativeRange.habitat: Steep outer reef slopes at depths of roughly 12–35 m; found on or near the surface of hard corals.

diet.notes: Tends to accept a variety of frozen and prepared meaty foods including brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, and finely chopped fresh seafood. Feeding twice daily has been recommended; live rock may serve as a supplemental foraging source.

avoidWith: large fish that may consume them

imageCandidates.notes: Rob (Wikimedia Commons), ~4000px original (1280px shown). Sharp in-situ reef shot of a whole live individual with translucent body, bold red vertical bars and gold-rimmed eye clearly visible; best diagnostic match.
呂一起 (Lyu yi-chi), 1102x768. Sharp oblique in-situ shot on hard coral showing full body, red bars and gold-rimmed eyes.
呂一起 (Lyu yi-chi), 2048x1536. Front-facing in-situ shot on coral, red barring and gold eye-rings clear; different angle for variety.

summary: The Red Striped Goby (Trimma cana) is a tiny saltwater goby reaching roughly 2.5 cm, with a nearly translucent body patterned with bold red vertical bars and large gold-rimmed eyes. Generally considered reef-safe and suitable for nano reef aquariums, it tends to settle well when kept in a small group introduced all at once.
careNotes: The Red Striped Goby is one of the smallest gobies available in the saltwater hobby, commonly sold at under 2.5 cm. Its elongated, nearly translucent body is patterned with bold red vertical bars and large gold-rimmed eyes — traits that make it easy to identify at a glance. In the wild, the species has been observed living solitarily or in small groups on the surfaces of hard corals along steep outer reef slopes. In captivity, it tends to settle comfortably in small groups or pairs when multiple specimens are introduced to the aquarium at the same time; conspecific aggression (hostility toward others of the same species) tends to remain low in tanks with adequate space and sufficient rockwork for retreat. Like most small gobies, it is considered an escape risk, and a tightly sealed aquarium lid is strongly advisable. The species is generally considered reef-safe.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for scientific name confirmation, distribution (Philippines, Caroline Is., Fiji, Marshall Is., Palau), taxonomy (Gobiidae / Gobiiformes), max size (2.5 cm SL), and wild temperature range (26.3–29°C). Wild temperature range differs from LiveAquaria recommendation (22.2–25.6°C); averaged range used and dataStatus set to needs_review.
